feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Passing dynamic values to Input parameters for CDS view for Data extraction from Datashere
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are looking for a solution to pass the dynamic values to the Input Parameters of the CDS view C_InvtryTurnoverAnalysis for the data extraction to Datasphere. We are using this SAP-provided CDS view C_InvtryTurnoverAnalysis in a custom CDS view and passing the static values for the dates currently to extract the Inventory Turnover every month. We did try passing the Date Functions to the Input parameters and getting the syntax errors. SAP Datasphere also has not provided the feature yet to extract the data from CDS views that have Input Parameters.

Below is the Date functions used to pass the dynamic previous month value to the Input parameter in the Custom CDS view which is using C_InvtryTurnoverAnalysis and throwing the syntax error.

P_StartDate : cast(
concat(
substring(
cast( add_months( $session.system_date, -1 ) as abap.char(8) ),
1,
6
),
'01'
)
as abap.dats
)

Following is the date functions tried to pass to the input parameters and getting the syntax errors.

P_StartDate : cast( concat( substring( cast( DATS_ADD_MONTHS( $session.system_date, -1) as abap.char(8) ), 1, 6 ), '01') as abap.dats ),

P_EndDate : DATS_ADD_DAYS(DATS_ADD_MONTHS(cast( concat( substring( cast( DATS_ADD_MONTHS( $session.system_date, -1 ) as abap.char(8) ), 1, 6 ), '01') as abap.dats ),1),-1)

Appreciate if you have come across the similar scenario and will help if any recommendations.

SAP Datasphere ABAP Development SAP BTP ABAP environment

entry.title: [SAC Trial] Authorization Request Error when connecting to SAP Datasphere (OData Connection)
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently trying to create an OData connection from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC Trial – AP11) to SAP Datasphere.

However, I am facing an issue during the OAuth authentication step.

Environment:

  • SAC URL: https://academy-t-sac.ap11.hcs.clod.sap
  • Datasphere URL: https://academydatasphere-1.ap11.hcs.cloud.sap

Connection Configuration:

  • Authentication Type: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code
  • Data Service URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.ap11.hcs.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/SPACE_00005323/Analytic_Model_GE298598
  • Token URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.authentication.ap11.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
  • Authorization URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.authentication.ap11.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize
  • OAuth Client ID: (from SAP Learning guide)
  • Secret: (from SAP Learning guide)

Issue:
When I click "Create", SAC redirects to the authorization URL but shows the following error:

What I have tried:

  • Switched endpoints from academydatasphere-1 to academy
  • Verified Space ID (SPACE_00005323)
  • Checked Data Service URL format
  • Tested authorization endpoint directly in browser

Questions:

  1. Are the academydatasphere-1 endpoints still valid for AP11 trial?
  2. Has the OAuth client (from SAP Learning material) expired or changed?
  3. Is there any required redirect URI configuration for SAC trial connections?
  4. What is the correct configuration for current SAP Datasphere + SAC trial landscape?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Why is my Valid OData Connection not visible in Connections in DataSphere DataFlow
entry.content:
Hello,

I've defined a valid OData connection from SAP DS towards our MS Graph https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/. with a Oath2.0 authorization.

When testing the connection with Validate this is OK, but whe I look into Connections in a DataFlow it doesn't appear ..

Any suggestions ?

entry.title: Does Sap datasphere supports transferring wirting outbound data to snowflake table
entry.content:
We are evaluating SAP Datasphere / SAP Business Data Cloud capabilities for data integration with Snowflake.

From the SAP documentation, we understand that Snowflake is supported in SAP Datasphere via Open Connectors. However, the documentation appears to describe Open Connectors primarily as inbound data sources into Datasphere, i.e., for reading or ingesting data.

Clarification requested

Can SAP Datasphere natively support writing or loading data into Snowflake tables, for example:

  • Persisting data from Datasphere into Snowflake tables
  • Supporting batch or scheduled loads
  • Acting as an outbound integration target for Snowflake

If this is supported:

  • What SAP‑recommended mechanism enables this?

If this is not supported:

  • Is Datasphere intended to be consumption‑only with respect to Snowflake, with outbound persistence handled by other SAP services?

We are looking for product‑level clarification on SAP Datasphere capabilities (not solution design or consulting guidance).

Any official clarification or experience‑based insights would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Datasphereにてアルファ変換を実施する
entry.content:
S/4のアルファ変換のような動作を行いたいのですがDatasphereにアルファ変換は機能として実現できますでしょうか？

実現したいことはDatasphereのローカルテーブルにCSVファイルでアップロードされたデータに対して前ゼロ無しでアップロードされたデータに対して特定の項目、特定の条件の場合に前ゼロ有にするような処理をすることを目的としています。

entry.title: Cannot Connect to SAP Datasphere Space by ODBC, asking provide X509 certificate
entry.content:
I am trying to connect to my SAP Datasphere Space database via ODBC, and I am getting the following error: "Provide X509 certificate file." Could someone let me know where I can get the file it's asking for? Or If anyone can tell me if we can disable this option of asking certificate and use only username and password, then it will be ok

 

**Note: This error is coming when i connect from Alteryx an ETL tool.**

**Update 1: I checked the odbc connection from my system and it is showing connection succesful both in windows 64-bit ODBC manager and also in Alteryx ETL, but when I query from the Alteryx Input tool it throws the same error above**


**WINDOWS 64-bit ODBC STATUS**

 

**Alteryx Data Connection Manager (DCM) STATUS**

 

**WHEN USE INPUT TOOL FOR THE DCM IN ALTERYX SHOWING ERROR**

entry.title: DataSphere OData Connection Paging
entry.content:
Hello,

I've managed tocreate a OData connection from DS to MSGraph, loading our AAD users and so on would be the goal.

The connection is working for remote tables, I can see the tables and inport them, but when fetching the data I'm always getting this error.

internal error: DPAdapterResultSet::Fetch:connection to graph.microsoft.com:443 returned status 400 message: {"error":{"code":"Request_UnsupportedQuery","message":"Invalid page size specified: '1000'. Must be between 1 and 999 inclusive.","innerError":{"date":"2026-04-09T07:55:56","request-id":"ead338c2-xxxx-481f-xxxx-2b5321c4c2de","client-request-id":"ead338c2-xxxx-481f-xxxx-2b5321c4c2de"}}}

Iv' specified in the ODataconnection extra headers

But somehow this is being ignored, anybody has an idea what's wrong ?

entry.title: SAP DataSphere – Moving Objects: Table, Dataflow from one Space to another Space.
entry.content:
During a recent implementation, an observation was made while moving objects between spaces in   After transporting a view from one space to another, field names were automatically converted to uppercase.

This post highlights the reason behind this behavior and its impact.

⸻

What Happened?
• A view was moved from a source space to a target space
• After deployment in the target space, all field names appeared in uppercase
• Previously, the field names were in mixed case (e.g., AssetNumber)

⸻

Why Does This Happen?


• Objects are not directly copied between spaces
• They are exported and re-imported into the target space
• During this process, objects are re-created and deployed again
• Metadata is regenerated, and system standardization is applied
• This results in technical field names being converted to uppercase

entry.title: How to Connect HR Non SAP System to BDC Data Sphere to See the Analytics Model and Views in SAC
entry.content:
Hi  Team,

How to Connect HR Non-SAP System to BDC Data Sphere is a client requirement. Would you kindly assist in providing the necessary documents Links and blogs for the same?  It would be beneficial to work on the Project.

Thanks & Regards,
 BASIS SUPPORT.

entry.title: Not able to post any blog in - 'Technology Blog Posts by Members'
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, 

I used to create blogs under 'Technology Blog Posts by Members' and that option is disabled now.

Can someone or SAP, advise on the solution ?

 

Alternatively tried to select from Board selector but only 'Technology Q&A' is available, nothing related to blogs.

 

Thanks

Lokesh

entry.title: Replication of ACDOCA, how fast can we go
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a replication-flow enabled on the I_GLACCOUNTLINEITEMRAWDATA, the OCDOCA view with CDC.

We do have some month closure reports working directly on that Local Table in DS, but as we can't define a fix schedule the flow run (you can define a interval) the next question that pop up, can we put this to every minute durng the first days of the month.

I've tried already every 5 minutes and our production servers didn't even 'sense' it, it did not put any extra load on the S4/HANA DB.

What intervals have you guys ever setup ?

 

KR, Harry

entry.title: Configuration Differences When SAP Datasphere Is Integrated into SAP BDC vs. Standalone
entry.content:
Initially, the landscape was as follows:
ECC → SAP LT → SAP Cloud Connector → SAP Datasphere → Azure Data Lake

However, the landscape has now changed to:
ECC → SAP LT → SAP Cloud Connector → SAP Datasphere (integrated into SAP BDC) → Azure Data Lake

Question:
Are there any configuration differences in SAP Cloud Connector, SAP Datasphere (integrated into SAP BDC), and Azure Data Lake compared to the setup when using SAP Datasphere as a standalone system?

entry.title: SAP Datasphere: Configuration Differences (BDC Integrated vs. Standalone)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

What are the configuration differences between SAP Datasphere (integrated into SAP BDC) and SAP Datasphere when used as a standalone version?
 
Thank you,
QuocDT5

entry.title: How do I get Object change user activity for Auditing in Datasphere?
entry.content: Hi, I am trying to get the user activity data in Datasphere, who made the changes to the space objects like views and models. I have followed the steps described in the SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3578156  to enable audit logging. After enabling the Audit in the space and System --> Configuration --> Audit. I can't find the view AUDIT_LOG in the DWC_AUDIT_READER schema. However, I can see the views below. Can you please confirm the AUDIT_LOG view exists? If yes, why can't I find it?

entry.title: DSP - High memory consumption by space $$GLOBAL$$
entry.content:
Hi,

What does this space $$GLOBAL$$  Do-   seeing high Memory consumption  periodically

Thanks

Anthony

entry.title: Smart Features (Smart Insights / Just Ask) with Fiscal Period Models in SAC – Any Workarounds?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m currently working on several FP&A and consolidation use cases in SAP Analytics Cloud where models are built using Fiscal Period / Fiscal Year dimensions (standard setup aligned with S/4HANA and finance processes).

As expected, we are facing the known limitation:
Smart Features (Smart Insights, Just Ask, Search to Insight) are not supported on models using fiscal time settings

Have you implemented any effective workaround or design pattern to enable or approximate Smart Features on fiscal-based models?

Specifically interested in:

  • Use of parallel calendar-based models → how do you manage reconciliation/governance?
  • Any approach leveraging Data Analyzer / calculated measures to mimic Smart Insights behavior?
  • Experience using Datasphere transformations to bridge fiscal ↔ calendar time for AI use cases
  • Trade-offs you’ve seen between accuracy vs usability

Context

In our architecture:

  • Planning and reporting models (Rev&Exp, Consolidation, Workforce, etc.) are fully fiscal-based
  • Data is sourced via SAP Datasphere and aligned with finance calendars
  • Business users (FP&A, Controllers) are increasingly asking for augmented analytics capabilities directly on these models

Appreciate any insights, patterns, or lessons learned from your implementations.

Thanks in advance.

SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Datasphere SAP Analytics Cloud, augmented analytics

entry.title: How to create a Tunnel connection to Datasphere in SAC?
entry.content:
How to create a Tunnel connection to Datasphere in SAC?
 

Please follow the step-by-step on the SAP Help Page: Live Data Connections to SAP Datasphere
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/ad4281e2875949f0b4d45d1072ff4c38.html

As per "Procedure" step 4.

From the Connection Type drop-down list, select Tunnel.

And please mind that for tunnel connections, SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere users must be provisioned with the same IdP.

entry.title: Data persistence in Datasphere
entry.content: Hello, I am using the data persistence in Datasphere. Based on the log, the number of records inserted in the table inflates to about 178x so the initial number of records which is 750k becomes 134M. This also inflates the amount of memory being used. Has anyone encountered this? Anyone with data persistence knowledge can share anything more about it? Thank you!

entry.title: Is SAP Datasphere mandatory for SAP Business Data Cloud configuration?
entry.content:
Hi,

In these 2 scenarios:

  • If user just want SAP-managed data products and SAP Intelligence application but does not want SAP Datasphere.

  • If user just want to use BDC connect to share SAP managed data product to Databricks / Snowflake?

Is SAP Datasphere mandatory?

Thank you.

entry.title: Adjusting an existing Datasphere connection without affecting imported remote tables
entry.content:
Hi,

Could you help me with this question? We have an established connection via Generic JDBC between Datasphere and CRM that uses a PostgreSQL driver (org.postgresql.Driver with URL jdbc:postgresql:). The Postgres database was recently updated to a new version, and I adjusted that same connection. It's the same port, same tables, so I only adjusted the IP address and the new username and password. However, the existing remote tables, which were already imported from that connection, stopped replicating.

I tried the following:

- Creating a new test connection and importing a test table, and it replicates without problems.

- From the existing (and adjusted) connection, I imported another table, and it replicates without problems.

- I also tried stopping automatic replication, changing the source, and reconnecting to the same connection, and updating replication, but without success.

Apparently, the error is only... For existing remote tables originating from that configured source, the error message displayed is as follows:

Error: {"timestamp":"2026-04-21 11:10:30.7592660", "origin":"_sys_task", "message":"internal error: DPAdapterResultSet::Fetch:Camel execution failed. at ptime/query/plan_executor/dml/hex/qe_hex_search.cc:512; Statement: ,INSERT INTO \"XXXXX\".\"XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_CT\" (\"idprevisaovendas\", \"idparceiro\", \"idsafra\", \"idtabeladepreco\", \"idusuario\", \"idlocal\", \"idgrupoempresarial\", \"datacadastro\", \"idfilial\", \"integrado\", \"idcotacaoptax\", \"valorcotacaoptax\", \"idpvmoeda\", \"idpvtabelapreco\", \"valorcotacao\", \"chaveerp\", \"ano\") SELECT \"idprevisaovendas\", \"idparceiro\", \"idsafra\", \"idtabeladepreco\", \"idusuario\", \"idlocal\", \"idgrupoempresarial\", \"datacadastro\", \"idfilial\", \"integrado\", \"idcotacaoptax\", \"valorcotacaoptax\", \"idpvmoeda\", \"idpvtabelapreco\", \"valorcotacao\", \"chaveerp\", \"ano\" FROM (WITH _SYS_WITH_1 AS (SELECT \"idprevisaovendas\" , \"idparceiro\" , \"idsafra\" , \"idtabeladepreco\" , \"idusuario\" , \"idlocal\" , \"idgrupoempresarial\" , \"datacadastro\" , \"idfilial\" , \"integrado\" , \"idcotacaoptax\" , \"valorcotacaoptax\" , \"idpvmoeda\" , \"idpvtabelapreco\" , \"valorcotacao\" , \"chaveerp\" , \"ano\" FROM \"XXXXX\".\"XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT\" AS \"XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT\") , _SYS_WITH_0 AS (SELECT \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idprevisaovendas\" AS \"idprevisaovendas\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idparceiro\" AS \"idparceiro\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idsafra\" AS \"idsafra\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idtabeladepreco\" AS \"idtabeladepreco\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idusuario\" AS \"idusuario\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idlocal\" AS \"idlocal\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idgrupoempresarial\" AS \"idgrupoempresarial\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"datacadastro\" AS \"datacadastro\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idfilial\" AS \"idfilial\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"integrado\" AS \"integrado\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public."} 

The goal is to avoid creating a new connection, as that would require re-importing all the remote tables. I already have views and models created using those tables, and I'm unsure how this would impact things. Is it possible to avoid creating a new connection?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards!
